definitions.statutory_reference: Sections 89–95, Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016

section_89_general_contravention.offence_type: Contravention of any provision of the Act or rules made thereunder.

penalty.subsequent_violation: Fine not less than ₹50,000 and may extend to ₹5,00,000.

section_90_offences_by_companies.offence_type: Offence committed by a company or establishment.
section_90_offences_by_companies.liability: Persons in charge and responsible for conduct of business deemed guilty unless due diligence proven.

section_91_fraudulent_benefits.offence_type: Fraudulently availing benefits meant for persons with benchmark disabilities.
section_91_fraudulent_benefits.penalty: Imprisonment up to 2 years or fine up to ₹1,00,000 or both.

section_92_atrocities_against_person_wit.offence_type: Intentional insult, intimidation, assault, sexual exploitation, denial of food or rights.
section_92_atrocities_against_person_wit.penalty: Imprisonment not less than 6 months which may extend to 5 years and fine.

section_93_failure_to_furnish_informatio.offence_type: Failure to produce required documents or information.

section_94_false_statement.offence_type: Making false statements or furnishing false information.

section_95_cognizance_of_offences.note: Court shall take cognizance only on complaint made by appropriate Government or authorized authority.

legal_boundary_conditions: Penalties apply only upon formal contravention.
Criminal liability requires statutory conditions to be satisfied.
